articles.headline: India Unveils Ambitious 'Green Growth 2.0' Roadmap on World Environment Day
articles.explainer:
• On World Environment Day 2025, India launched an enhanced 'Green Growth 2.0' roadmap, signaling a deeper commitment to sustainable development and climate action.
• The new roadmap emphasizes scaling up Mission LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) initiatives, promoting behavioral changes for sustainable living across all sections of society.
• Significant focus is placed on expanding the Green Credit Program, incentivizing voluntary environmental actions by individuals and organizations through a market-based mechanism.
• India reiterated its accelerated targets for renewable energy deployment, aiming for substantial increase in non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030, aligning with its Net Zero commitments.
• The plan also includes enhanced international cooperation for climate finance and technology transfer, positioning India as a leader in global environmental stewardship.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Environmental impact assessment
Conservation
Climate change
Sustainable development
prelimsPoints: World Environment Day is observed annually on June 5th.
Mission LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) was launched by India in 2022.
The Green Credit Program aims to incentivize environmental actions.
India's updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) include targets for renewable energy capacity.
The concept of 'Green Growth' integrates economic growth with environmental sustainability.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in global climate action and environmental conservation.
Analyze the potential of Mission LiFE and Green Credit Program in fostering sustainable behavioral changes.
Evaluate the economic and environmental implications of India's accelerated renewable energy targets.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in implementing a comprehensive 'Green Growth' strategy in India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a green leaf intertwined with gears, symbolizing sustainable economic growth.
articles.trivia: The first World Environment Day was celebrated in 1974 with the theme 'Only One Earth'.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Emphasizes Right to Privacy
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, dismissing several petitions challenging its provisions.
• The court emphasized the critical balance struck by the Act between individual's right to privacy and legitimate state interests, including national security and public order.
• The ruling clarified the responsibilities of data fiduciaries and the rights of data principals, reinforcing mechanisms for data protection and grievance redressal.
• Legal experts note the judgment provides much-needed clarity on India's data governance framework, aligning it with global best practices while addressing unique domestic contexts.
• The decision is expected to accelerate the full implementation of the Act, impacting various sectors from technology to finance in how they handle personal data.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Fundamental rights
Judiciary
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) was enacted in 2023.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right.
Data Fiduciary refers to any person who determines the purpose and means of processing personal data.
The Act includes provisions for data breach notification and a Data Protection Board of India.
The Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze how the Supreme Court's ruling on the DPDP Act impacts the balance between individual rights and state interests.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in implementing a robust data protection framework in a digitally transforming India.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in interpreting and upholding fundamental rights in the context of emerging technologies.
Examine the implications of the DPDP Act for businesses and citizens in India's digital economy.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents with binary code flowing in the background, symbolizing digital law.
articles.trivia: India's data protection journey gained significant momentum after the Srikrishna Committee report in 2018.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and related legislation

articles.headline: Gati Shakti Master Plan Accelerates Multi-Modal Infrastructure Development, Boosts Economic Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry reported significant progress under the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan, accelerating multi-modal infrastructure projects across the country.
• The integrated planning platform has enabled seamless coordination among various ministries, reducing project delays and optimizing resource allocation for critical infrastructure.
• Key achievements include faster execution of highway projects, expansion of railway networks, development of new ports, and enhanced logistics efficiency.
• The plan's emphasis on data-driven decision-making and geospatial mapping has led to more efficient project alignment and reduced environmental impact.
• Economic analysts attribute the improved infrastructure to a positive impact on manufacturing, exports, and overall economic growth, creating new employment opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan was launched in October 2021.
It aims to break departmental silos and bring various ministries together for integrated planning.
The plan uses geospatial technology for mapping and planning infrastructure projects.
It covers 16 ministries, including Railways, Roadways, Shipping, Aviation, Petroleum, Power, and Telecom.
The objective is to reduce logistics costs and improve global competitiveness.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan in achieving its objectives of integrated infrastructure development and logistics efficiency.
Discuss how multi-modal connectivity under Gati Shakti can transform India's manufacturing sector and boost exports.
Analyze the challenges encountered in the implementation of large-scale infrastructure projects under Gati Shakti and suggest potential solutions.
Examine the socio-economic impact of accelerated infrastructure development on regional disparities and employment generation.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of interconnected highways, railways, and ports, symbolizing multi-modal logistics.
articles.trivia: The Gati Shakti platform integrates over 200 layers of geospatial data to provide comprehensive planning tools.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD) for Future Space Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
• This test marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aiming to drastically reduce the cost of space access.
• The RLV-TD demonstrated advanced hypersonic flight capabilities, autonomous landing, and re-entry maneuvers, validating critical technologies.
• ISRO Chairman highlighted the indigenous development of these technologies, underscoring India's growing self-reliance in cutting-edge space research.
• The successful demonstration paves the way for future operational reusable launch vehicles, enhancing India's competitive edge in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
prelimsPoints: RLV-TD stands for Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator.
The primary goal of reusable launch vehicles is to reduce the cost of space launches.
ISRO's RLV-TD program aims to develop a two-stage to orbit (TSTO) reusable launch vehicle.
Key technologies demonstrated include hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and re-entry heat shield.
India is one of the few countries actively pursuing reusable launch vehicle technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's future space missions and its implications for global space competition.
Analyze how indigenous development of RLV-TD contributes to India's self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in the space sector.
Examine the technical challenges involved in developing reusable launch vehicles and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the potential economic benefits of reduced launch costs for satellite deployment and space tourism.
articles.imageDescription: An artist's impression of ISRO's RLV-TD gliding back to Earth, with the Indian flag visible.
articles.trivia: The RLV-TD program is part of ISRO's long-term vision to make space access more affordable and reliable.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Defence Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and France held their annual Strategic Dialogue, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region and strengthening defence ties.
• Discussions centered on maritime security, counter-terrorism efforts, and promoting a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific order.
• Both nations explored avenues for greater defence technology transfer and joint production, building on existing strategic partnerships.
• The dialogue also touched upon global challenges, including climate change and multilateral reforms, reiterating a shared vision for a rules-based international order.
• This engagement underscores the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and France in addressing regional and global security dynamics.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
India and its neighborhood- relations
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and France share a long-standing strategic partnership established in 1998.
France is a key partner for India in the Indo-Pacific region.
Defence cooperation includes joint exercises like Varuna (Navy), Garuda (Air Force), and Shakti (Army).
Both countries are proponents of a multi-polar world order.
Recent defence deals include Rafale fighter jets and Scorpene submarines.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in promoting stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various dimensions of defence cooperation between India and France and its implications for India's strategic autonomy.
Evaluate how India and France are aligning their approaches to address global challenges such as climate change and multilateral reforms.
Examine the potential for future collaboration between India and France in emerging areas like space, cyber security, and critical technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France prominently displayed against a backdrop of naval ships, symbolizing defence cooperation.
articles.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India after the 1998 nuclear tests.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Completes 52 Years, India Reports Steady Increase in Big Cat Population
articles.explainer:
• India today celebrated 52 years of Project Tiger, a landmark conservation program, with the release of a report indicating a continued steady increase in the country's tiger population.
• The latest census data highlights the success of stringent conservation measures, habitat protection, and anti-poaching efforts across various tiger reserves.
• Despite the overall positive trend, the report flagged concerns regarding human-wildlife conflict in buffer zones and the need for enhanced corridor connectivity.
• Union Environment Minister reiterated India's commitment to the 'Save Tiger' mission, emphasizing community participation and sustainable tourism practices.
• India currently hosts over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, solidifying its position as a global leader in tiger conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
The latest tiger census (2022) reported 3,682 tigers in India.
India accounts for approximately 70% of the world's tiger population.
National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is the statutory body for tiger conservation.
Tiger reserves are legally established under the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key strategies and successes of Project Tiger over the past five decades in conserving the tiger population in India.
Analyze the ongoing challenges in tiger conservation, including human-wildlife conflict and habitat fragmentation, and suggest mitigation measures.
Evaluate the role of community participation and eco-tourism in promoting sustainable tiger conservation efforts.
Examine how tiger conservation contributes to broader biodiversity protection and ecological balance in India.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger walking through dense forest foliage.
articles.trivia: The tiger (Panthera tigris) is India's national animal.

articles.headline: Centre Launches 'Jan Samarth 2.0' Portal for Streamlined Access to Government Schemes
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched 'Jan Samarth 2.0', an upgraded national portal designed to provide a single-window platform for various government schemes and services.
• This enhanced version aims to further simplify the application process, reduce paperwork, and improve transparency in scheme delivery for citizens and small businesses.
• The portal integrates more schemes from different ministries, offering personalized recommendations based on user eligibility and requirements.
• It leverages advanced analytics and AI to enhance user experience, providing real-time tracking of applications and digital verification processes.
• Jan Samarth 2.0 is a significant step towards achieving the goals of digital governance and financial inclusion, ensuring last-mile delivery of public services.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Jan Samarth Portal was originally launched in June 2022.
It is a national portal for credit-linked government schemes.
The portal aims to provide digital, end-to-end solutions for various schemes.
It covers schemes related to education, agriculture, business, and livelihood.
The platform is managed by the Ministry of Finance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of e-governance initiatives like Jan Samarth 2.0 in enhancing public service delivery and promoting transparency in India.
Analyze how such integrated platforms contribute to financial inclusion and the ease of living for citizens and small entrepreneurs.
Examine the challenges in ensuring digital literacy and accessibility for all sections of society to fully leverage e-governance portals.
Evaluate the potential of AI and advanced analytics in further streamlining government services and improving efficiency.
articles.imageDescription: A smartphone screen displaying the Jan Samarth portal interface with various scheme icons.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'single-window clearance' is a key component of India's ease of doing business reforms.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Agri-Tech Start-ups with New Incubation Fund to Modernize Farming
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced the establishment of a new incubation fund dedicated to supporting agri-tech start-ups across India.
• The fund aims to foster innovation in agriculture, encouraging the development of solutions for precision farming, supply chain optimization, and market linkages.
• This initiative is part of the government's broader strategy to modernize the agricultural sector, enhance farmer income, and ensure food security through technological intervention.
• Selected start-ups will receive financial assistance, mentorship, and access to agricultural research facilities and farmer networks.
• The move is expected to attract more young entrepreneurs to agriculture, driving sustainable and efficient farming practices.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Food processing and related industries in India- scope and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: Agri-tech refers to the application of technology in agriculture.
The government has initiatives like Agri-Startup Conclave and Accelerator Program for agri-tech.
Precision farming uses technologies like IoT, AI, and drones.
Improving farmer income is a key objective of agricultural reforms.
Incubation funds provide early-stage funding and support to start-ups.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of agri-tech start-ups in addressing the challenges faced by the Indian agricultural sector, such as productivity, market access, and climate change.
Analyze how government support through incubation funds can foster innovation and entrepreneurship in agriculture.
Examine the role of technology in enhancing farmer income and ensuring food security in India.
Evaluate the challenges in integrating modern agri-tech solutions with traditional farming practices and suggest strategies for effective adoption.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer using a tablet in a field, with a drone flying overhead, symbolizing modern agriculture.
articles.trivia: India's agricultural sector contributes significantly to its GDP and employs a large portion of the workforce.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Global South Priorities at G20 Development Working Group Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India actively participated in the G20 Development Working Group (DWG) meeting, strongly advocating for the priorities and concerns of the Global South.
• Discussions focused on accelerating progress towards Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly in areas like climate finance, food security, and debt relief for developing nations.
• India emphasized the need for reformed multilateral institutions that are more representative and responsive to the needs of emerging economies.
• The Indian delegation highlighted its own successful development models and offered to share best practices with other Global South countries.
• This engagement reinforces India's commitment to multilateralism and its growing voice as a champion for developing nations on the global stage.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The G20 Development Working Group (DWG) focuses on development issues.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023, emphasizing 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (One Earth, One Family, One Future).
The concept of 'Global South' refers to developing countries, primarily in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.
Climate finance refers to local, national or transnational financing, which may be drawn from public, private and alternative sources of financing.
India has consistently championed the cause of developing countries in international forums.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's increasing role as a voice for the Global South in international forums like the G20.
Analyze the key challenges faced by developing countries in achieving Sustainable Development Goals and how India's advocacy can help.
Examine the need for reforms in global multilateral institutions to make them more inclusive and equitable for the Global South.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's development cooperation models in promoting south-south cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: A globe highlighting the countries of the Global South, with overlapping hands symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: India organized the 'Voice of Global South Summit' in January 2023 to bring together developing countries.

articles.headline: Cybersecurity Framework Strengthened Amidst Rising Digital Threats to Critical Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) announced a significant upgrade to India's cybersecurity framework following a surge in digital threats.
• The enhanced framework focuses on real-time threat intelligence sharing, improved incident response protocols, and deeper public-private partnerships in critical sectors.
• New guidelines were issued for safeguarding critical infrastructure, including energy, finance, and telecommunications, against sophisticated cyber-attacks.
• The government also launched a nationwide awareness campaign to educate organizations and individuals about best practices for cyber hygiene and data protection.
• These measures aim to fortify India's digital defenses and ensure the resilience of its vital services in an increasingly interconnected world.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkage of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: NCIIPC (National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre) is the nodal agency for CII protection.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) is defined under the Information Technology Act, 2000.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for cyber security incident response.
Cyber-attacks can target sectors like power grids, banking systems, and communication networks.
Public-private partnership is crucial for effective cybersecurity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats to India's critical infrastructure and the challenges in safeguarding them.
Analyze the key components of India's strengthened cybersecurity framework and its effectiveness in mitigating digital risks.
Examine the role of international cooperation and public-private partnerships in building a resilient cybersecurity ecosystem.
Evaluate the importance of cyber hygiene and digital literacy for citizens in bolstering national cybersecurity.
articles.imageDescription: A digital shield icon overlaying a network of interconnected servers, symbolizing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries experiencing a high volume of cyber-attacks annually.

articles.headline: National Health Mission's 'Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission' Achieves Key Milestones
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced that the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has achieved significant milestones in digitalizing India's healthcare ecosystem.
• Over 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) have been created, enabling citizens to manage their digital health records securely.
• The mission has expanded its network of healthcare professionals and facilities registered on the Health Professional Registry (HPR) and Health Facility Registry (HFR).
• Telemedicine services have seen a substantial uptake, especially in remote areas, improving accessibility to quality healthcare consultations.
• ABDM is transforming healthcare delivery by ensuring interoperability of health data and promoting a more efficient, patient-centric system.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) was launched nationwide in September 2021.
ABHA ID (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) is a 14-digit unique health identifier.
The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for healthcare services.
Telemedicine involves providing clinical healthcare at a distance using technology.
Interoperability in healthcare allows different systems to exchange and make use of data.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in creating an inclusive and efficient healthcare system in India.
Analyze the challenges in ensuring data privacy and security within the ABDM framework and suggest measures to address them.
Evaluate the impact of telemedicine expansion under ABDM on healthcare accessibility, especially in rural and underserved areas.
Examine how digital health records and interoperability can improve patient outcomes and streamline healthcare delivery.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of healthcare workers, digital devices, and a patient, symbolizing digital health.
articles.trivia: India's National Health Policy 2017 envisioned a digital health ecosystem to achieve Universal Health Coverage.

articles.headline: India Explores Green Hydrogen Export Potential with New Production Incentives
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced new production-linked incentives (PLI) for green hydrogen and electrolyzer manufacturing.
• This move aims to boost domestic production capabilities and position India as a global hub for green hydrogen export.
• Green hydrogen, produced using renewable energy, is seen as a crucial component of India's energy transition strategy towards achieving Net Zero emissions.
• The incentives are designed to attract significant investments in the sector, create jobs, and reduce the cost of green hydrogen production.
• Experts believe that with its abundant renewable energy resources, India has the potential to become a leading player in the global green hydrogen economy.
gsSubTopics: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission was approved by the Union Cabinet in January 2023.
Electrolyzers are devices that split water into hydrogen and oxygen.
PLI schemes are designed to boost domestic manufacturing and exports.
India aims to achieve Net Zero emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and its commitment to achieving Net Zero emissions.
Analyze the role of Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes in fostering the growth of the green hydrogen sector in India.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in positioning India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Evaluate the potential impact of green hydrogen on various sectors of the Indian economy, including industry, transport, and agriculture.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized diagram showing an electrolyzer powered by wind turbines and solar panels, producing hydrogen.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe.

articles.headline: Monsoon Preparedness Review Highlights Focus on Climate-Resilient Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) conducted a review of monsoon preparedness, emphasizing the need for climate-resilient agricultural practices.
• With predictions of varying rainfall patterns, states were urged to implement early warning systems and promote drought-resistant crop varieties.
• Focus was also placed on enhancing water conservation measures, including rainwater harvesting and efficient irrigation techniques, to mitigate flood and drought risks.
• The review highlighted the importance of inter-agency coordination and community-level preparedness for effective disaster management during the monsoon season.
• This proactive approach aims to safeguard agricultural livelihoods and ensure food security amidst changing climatic conditions.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Environmental impact assessment

Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
prelimsPoints: Monsoon is a seasonal reversal of wind direction that causes significant rainfall in India.
NDMA (National Disaster Management Authority) is the apex body for disaster management in India.
Climate-resilient agriculture aims to adapt farming systems to climate change impacts.
Early warning systems provide timely information about impending hazards.
Rainwater harvesting is a method of collecting and storing rainwater for future use.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges posed by erratic monsoon patterns to Indian agriculture and the need for climate-resilient strategies.
Analyze the role of early warning systems and community-based disaster management in enhancing preparedness for monsoon-related hazards.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various water conservation measures in mitigating the impacts of floods and droughts on agricultural productivity.
Examine the policy interventions required to promote the adoption of climate-resilient agricultural practices among farmers in India.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting healthy crops under a cloudy sky, with a rain gauge nearby.
articles.trivia: The Indian monsoon typically accounts for about 70-90% of the country's annual rainfall.

articles.headline: RBI Mandates Enhanced Cyber Security Measures for Cooperative Banks
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) issued new directives mandating enhanced cybersecurity measures for all cooperative banks, including urban and rural cooperative banks.
• The move comes amidst increasing digitization of banking services and a rise in cyber-attacks targeting financial institutions.
• The directives include implementing robust IT governance frameworks, conducting regular cybersecurity audits, and establishing dedicated cyber incident response teams.
• RBI emphasized the importance of safeguarding customer data and ensuring the resilience of banking operations against evolving cyber threats.
• This regulatory push aims to strengthen the overall financial stability and security of the cooperative banking sector.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkage of organized crime with terrorism.
Basics of cyber security
prelimsPoints: Cooperative banks are governed by both banking regulations (RBI) and cooperative laws (state/central).
RBI is the central banking institution of India.
Cybersecurity audits assess the security posture of an organization's systems.
IT governance refers to the framework for managing and monitoring IT resources.
Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC) provides insurance cover for bank deposits.
mainsPoints: Discuss the unique challenges faced by cooperative banks in adopting advanced cybersecurity measures and the implications of RBI's new directives.
Analyze the importance of a robust cybersecurity framework in maintaining financial stability and consumer trust in the digital banking era.
Examine the balance between regulatory oversight and operational flexibility for cooperative banks in implementing cybersecurity mandates.
Evaluate the broader impact of enhancing cybersecurity in the cooperative banking sector on financial inclusion and rural development.
articles.imageDescription: A lock icon superimposed on a bank building, representing financial cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: Cooperative banks play a crucial role in providing credit to agricultural and small-scale industries, especially in rural areas.

articles.headline: Inter-State Council Meeting Addresses Fiscal Devolution and Cooperative Federalism Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Inter-State Council convened a crucial meeting today to discuss issues pertaining to fiscal devolution, GST compensation, and strengthening cooperative federalism.
• State representatives raised concerns regarding the adequacy and timely transfer of funds from the Centre, particularly for centrally sponsored schemes.
• Deliberations focused on streamlining the Goods and Services Tax (GST) compensation mechanism and addressing revenue shortfalls experienced by some states.
• The Council emphasized the need for greater consultation between the Centre and states on policy formulation and resource allocation.
• This meeting underscored the ongoing efforts to foster a more balanced and collaborative Centre-State relationship in India's federal structure.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Comparison of the Indian constitutional scheme with that of other countries
prelimsPoints: The Inter-State Council is established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution.
It is a recommendatory body to investigate and discuss subjects of common interest between the Centre and states.
The Council is chaired by the Prime Minister.
Fiscal devolution refers to the transfer of financial powers and resources from the central government to state governments.
GST compensation cess was levied to compensate states for revenue losses arising from the implementation of GST.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of the Inter-State Council in strengthening cooperative federalism in India.
Analyze the key challenges in fiscal devolution and Centre-State financial relations, particularly concerning GST compensation and centrally sponsored schemes.
Evaluate the mechanisms for dispute resolution between the Centre and states in India's federal structure.
Examine how greater consultation and collaboration between the Centre and states can lead to more effective governance and policy implementation.
articles.imageDescription: A map of India with interconnected gears, symbolizing the federal structure and Centre-State cooperation.
articles.trivia: The Sarkaria Commission on Centre-State Relations (1983) strongly recommended the establishment of a permanent Inter-State Council.

articles.headline: India-Sri Lanka Maritime Security Dialogue Reinforces Regional Stability
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka held their annual Maritime Security Dialogue, focusing on enhanced cooperation to ensure peace and stability in the Indian Ocean Region.
• Discussions covered joint patrolling, intelligence sharing, capacity building for maritime law enforcement, and addressing issues like illegal fishing and drug trafficking.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to upholding international maritime law and freedom of navigation in critical sea lanes.
• The dialogue also explored avenues for greater collaboration in disaster response and humanitarian assistance in the region.
• This continued engagement highlights the importance of bilateral ties in securing the shared maritime domain and promoting regional prosperity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkage of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: India and Sri Lanka share a close maritime boundary.
The Palk Strait and Gulf of Mannar are significant maritime areas between the two countries.
India's SAGAR (Security And Growth for All in the Region) doctrine emphasizes maritime cooperation.
Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated (IUU) fishing is a major concern.
Joint exercises like SLINEX (Sri Lanka-India Naval Exercise) are conducted.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of India-Sri Lanka maritime security cooperation for regional stability in the Indian Ocean Region.
Analyze the common maritime challenges faced by India and Sri Lanka, such as illegal fishing and drug trafficking, and how bilateral cooperation addresses them.
Evaluate India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and SAGAR doctrine in the context of its relations with Sri Lanka.
Examine the potential for greater collaboration in areas like disaster response, humanitarian assistance, and sustainable ocean economy between the two nations.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka sailing together in the open sea.
articles.trivia: Sri Lanka is strategically located near major international shipping lanes in the Indian Ocean.

articles.headline: Indus-X Showcases Defence Innovation, Fostering US-India Collaboration
articles.explainer:
• The second edition of the 'Indus-X Summit' was held, bringing together defence innovators, start-ups, and industry leaders from India and the United States.
• The event showcased cutting-edge technologies and solutions in areas such as artificial intelligence, unmanned systems, cyber security, and advanced materials for defence applications.
• Indus-X aims to facilitate joint research and development, co-production, and co-development initiatives between the two countries.
• This collaboration is a key component of the iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies) framework, fostering a robust defence industrial ecosystem.
• The summit underscored the growing strategic alignment between India and the US in defence and technology, promoting interoperability and innovation.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkage of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Indus-X (India-US Defense Acceleration Ecosystem) was launched in June 2023.
It is a joint initiative under the iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies).
iCET aims to deepen strategic technology partnerships between India and the US.
The focus areas include AI, quantum computing, semiconductors, and defence innovation.
The US is a major defence partner for India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus-X initiative in fostering defence innovation and technological collaboration between India and the United States.
Analyze how the iCET framework is transforming the strategic technology partnership between India and the US.
Examine the potential benefits of co-production and co-development in defence for India's indigenous defence manufacturing capabilities.
Evaluate the implications of enhanced India-US defence technology cooperation for regional security and global power dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: A handshake between two individuals, with a background of circuit boards and military vehicles, symbolizing tech and defense collaboration.
articles.trivia: The iCET was announced during the visit of the US National Security Advisor to India in January 2023.

articles.headline: Urban Local Bodies Adopt Innovative Waste Management Solutions for Swachh Bharat Mission
articles.explainer:
• Several Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) across India were recognized for implementing innovative waste management solutions under the Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban 2.0.
• These initiatives include advanced waste-to-energy plants, decentralized composting units, and robust segregation-at-source programs.
• Focus has been on adopting circular economy principles, maximizing resource recovery, and minimizing landfill dependence.
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs highlighted successful models involving public-private partnerships and community participation.
• These efforts are crucial for achieving the goals of a 'Garbage-Free India' and improving urban sanitation and environmental health.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.

The mission aims to make Indian cities 'Garbage Free' and 'Water Secure'.

Waste-to-energy plants convert waste materials into usable energy.
Segregation at source is crucial for effective waste management.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Embraces Digital Transformation for Post-Pandemic Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism highlighted the ongoing digital transformation in India's tourism sector, aiming for robust post-pandemic growth.
• Initiatives include the development of a national digital tourism platform, promoting virtual tours, and leveraging AI for personalized travel experiences.
• Focus has been on enhancing online booking systems, digital payment infrastructure, and seamless connectivity for tourists.
• The 'Dekho Apna Desh' initiative continues to encourage domestic tourism, supported by digital marketing campaigns and unique experiential offerings.
• This digital push is expected to attract a new generation of travelers, improve service quality, and boost employment in the hospitality industry.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Tourism sector and its impact

The tourism sector is a significant contributor to India's GDP and employment.
Digitalization helps in seamless booking, contactless payments, and personalized services.
AI and data analytics can be used to understand traveler preferences and offer tailored packages.
The Ministry of Tourism is the nodal ministry for promoting tourism in India.

articles.headline: Startup India Initiative Expands to Tier 2 & 3 Cities, Fostering Local Entrepreneurship
articles.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) announced the expansion of the Startup India initiative to aggressively promote entrepreneurship in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
• This expansion includes setting up more incubation centers, providing mentorship, and offering seed funding specifically tailored for local innovations.
• The aim is to decentralize the startup ecosystem, tap into regional talent, and address specific local challenges through innovative solutions.
• Government programs will focus on creating a supportive regulatory environment and facilitating market access for emerging startups in smaller cities.
• This move is expected to boost local economies, create jobs, and foster a culture of innovation beyond major metropolitan areas.

Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life

DPIIT (Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade) is the nodal department for Startup India.

highlights: India launches 'Green Growth 2.0' roadmap on World Environment Day, emphasizing Mission LiFE and green finance.
Supreme Court upholds Digital Personal Data Protection Act, affirming the right to privacy and data governance.
ISRO successfully tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD), marking a crucial step towards affordable space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about World Environment Day (WED) and India's 'Green Growth 2.0' roadmap are correct?
1. World Environment Day is celebrated annually on June 5th.
2. The 'Green Growth 2.0' roadmap focuses on expanding Mission LiFE and Green Credit Program.
3. India aims to achieve Net Zero emissions by 2050 as part of this roadmap.
4. The Green Credit Program is a regulatory mechanism to penalize environmental violations.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: World Environment Day is indeed observed annually on June 5th. Statement 2 is correct: The 'Green Growth 2.0' roadmap, as discussed in the article, emphasizes scaling up Mission LiFE and expanding the Green Credit Program. Statement 3 is incorrect: India's target for achieving Net Zero emissions is by 2070, not 2050. Statement 4 is incorrect: The Green Credit Program aims to incentivize voluntary environmental actions, not primarily to penalize violations.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, as recently upheld by the Supreme Court:
1. The Act defines 'Data Fiduciary' as any person who determines the purpose and means of processing personal data.
2. The Supreme Court's ruling emphasizes the balance between an individual's right to privacy and legitimate state interests.
3. The Act establishes the National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) as the nodal authority for data protection.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The DPDP Act defines 'Data Fiduciary' as an entity determining the purpose and means of processing personal data. Statement 2 is correct: The Supreme Court's ruling indeed highlighted the balance between privacy rights and state interests. Statement 3 is incorrect: The DPDP Act establishes a Data Protection Board of India, not NCIIPC. NCIIPC is concerned with Critical Information Infrastructure protection, not the overall data protection framework under DPDP Act.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan, consider the following statements:
1. It was launched in 2021 with the aim of breaking departmental silos in infrastructure planning.
2. The plan utilizes geospatial technology and integrates data layers from 16 ministries.
3. Its primary objective is to boost agricultural exports by subsidizing logistics costs for farmers.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan was launched in October 2021 to bring various ministries together for integrated planning. Statement 2 is correct: It uses geospatial technology and integrates data from 16 ministries for efficient project alignment. Statement 3 is incorrect: While Gati Shakti aims to improve logistics and boost exports, its primary objective is not solely to subsidize agricultural exports, but rather to enhance multi-modal connectivity and infrastructure development across various sectors to reduce overall logistics costs and improve competitiveness.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following accurately describes the significance of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) program?
(a) It is primarily aimed at developing a manned mission to Mars by 2030.
(b) Its main goal is to reduce the cost of space access by developing fully reusable launch vehicles.
(c) It focuses on deploying a large constellation of low-Earth orbit communication satellites.
(d) It is designed for deep-space exploration missions to study exoplanets.
options: (a) It is primarily aimed at developing a manned mission to Mars by 2030.
(b) Its main goal is to reduce the cost of space access by developing fully reusable launch vehicles.
(c) It focuses on deploying a large constellation of low-Earth orbit communication satellites.
(d) It is designed for deep-space exploration missions to study exoplanets.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Its main goal is to reduce the cost of space access by developing fully reusable launch vehicles.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The RLV-TD program's core objective, as stated in the article and generally understood, is to develop technologies for reusable launch vehicles to significantly reduce the cost of space launches and make space access more affordable. Options (a), (c), and (d) describe other potential space objectives but not the primary focus of the RLV-TD program.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding Project Tiger in India:
1. Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to conserve the Bengal tiger population.
2. India is home to approximately 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
3. The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is a statutory body overseeing Project Tiger.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. Project Tiger was indeed launched in 1973. India holds about 70% of the global wild tiger population, a fact often highlighted. The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is a statutory body established under the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972, for strengthening tiger conservation.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss India's strategy for achieving its climate commitments, referencing recent initiatives like 'Green Growth 2.0' and Mission LiFE. What challenges does India face in balancing economic development with environmental sustainability? (15 marks, 250 words)

Conclude with India's leadership aspirations.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's decision upholding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, for individual privacy and state surveillance in India. GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Emphasizes Right to Privacy
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, dismissing several petitions challenging its provisions.
• The court emphasized the critical balance struck by the Act between individual's right to privacy and legitimate state interests, including national security and public order.
• The ruling clarified the responsibilities of data fiduciaries and the rights of data principals, reinforcing mechanisms for data protection and grievance redressal.
• Legal experts note the judgment provides much-needed clarity on India's data governance framework, aligning it with global best practices while addressing unique domestic contexts.
• The decision is expected to accelerate the full implementation of the Act, impacting various sectors from technology to finance in how they handle personal data.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Fundamental rights
Judiciary
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) was enacted in 2023.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right.
Data Fiduciary refers to any person who determines the purpose and means of processing personal data.
The Act includes provisions for data breach notification and a Data Protection Board of India.
The Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze how the Supreme Court's ruling on the DPDP Act impacts the balance between individual rights and state interests.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in implementing a robust data protection framework in a digitally transforming India.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in interpreting and upholding fundamental rights in the context of emerging technologies.
Examine the implications of the DPDP Act for businesses and citizens in India's digital economy.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents with binary code flowing in the background, symbolizing digital law.
GS2.trivia: India's data protection journey gained significant momentum after the Srikrishna Committee report in 2018.

GS2.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Defence Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and France held their annual Strategic Dialogue, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region and strengthening defence ties.
• Discussions centered on maritime security, counter-terrorism efforts, and promoting a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific order.
• Both nations explored avenues for greater defence technology transfer and joint production, building on existing strategic partnerships.
• The dialogue also touched upon global challenges, including climate change and multilateral reforms, reiterating a shared vision for a rules-based international order.
• This engagement underscores the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and France in addressing regional and global security dynamics.

Both countries are proponents of a multi-polar world order.
Recent defence deals include Rafale fighter jets and Scorpene submarines.

Discuss the various dimensions of defence cooperation between India and France and its implications for India's strategic autonomy.

GS2.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India after the 1998 nuclear tests.

GS2.headline: Centre Launches 'Jan Samarth 2.0' Portal for Streamlined Access to Government Schemes
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government launched 'Jan Samarth 2.0', an upgraded national portal designed to provide a single-window platform for various government schemes and services.
• This enhanced version aims to further simplify the application process, reduce paperwork, and improve transparency in scheme delivery for citizens and small businesses.
• The portal integrates more schemes from different ministries, offering personalized recommendations based on user eligibility and requirements.
• It leverages advanced analytics and AI to enhance user experience, providing real-time tracking of applications and digital verification processes.
• Jan Samarth 2.0 is a significant step towards achieving the goals of digital governance and financial inclusion, ensuring last-mile delivery of public services.

It is a national portal for credit-linked government schemes.
The portal aims to provide digital, end-to-end solutions for various schemes.
It covers schemes related to education, agriculture, business, and livelihood.
The platform is managed by the Ministry of Finance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of e-governance initiatives like Jan Samarth 2.0 in enhancing public service delivery and promoting transparency in India.

GS2.trivia: The concept of 'single-window clearance' is a key component of India's ease of doing business reforms.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Global South Priorities at G20 Development Working Group Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India actively participated in the G20 Development Working Group (DWG) meeting, strongly advocating for the priorities and concerns of the Global South.
• Discussions focused on accelerating progress towards Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly in areas like climate finance, food security, and debt relief for developing nations.
• India emphasized the need for reformed multilateral institutions that are more representative and responsive to the needs of emerging economies.
• The Indian delegation highlighted its own successful development models and offered to share best practices with other Global South countries.
• This engagement reinforces India's commitment to multilateralism and its growing voice as a champion for developing nations on the global stage.

India held the G20 Presidency in 2023, emphasizing 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (One Earth, One Family, One Future).

Climate finance refers to local, national or transnational financing, which may be drawn from public, private and alternative sources of financing.
India has consistently championed the cause of developing countries in international forums.

GS2.headline: National Health Mission's 'Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission' Achieves Key Milestones
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced that the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has achieved significant milestones in digitalizing India's healthcare ecosystem.
• Over 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) have been created, enabling citizens to manage their digital health records securely.
• The mission has expanded its network of healthcare professionals and facilities registered on the Health Professional Registry (HPR) and Health Facility Registry (HFR).
• Telemedicine services have seen a substantial uptake, especially in remote areas, improving accessibility to quality healthcare consultations.
• ABDM is transforming healthcare delivery by ensuring interoperability of health data and promoting a more efficient, patient-centric system.

ABHA ID (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) is a 14-digit unique health identifier.
The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for healthcare services.
Telemedicine involves providing clinical healthcare at a distance using technology.
Interoperability in healthcare allows different systems to exchange and make use of data.

GS2.headline: Inter-State Council Meeting Addresses Fiscal Devolution and Cooperative Federalism Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• The Inter-State Council convened a crucial meeting today to discuss issues pertaining to fiscal devolution, GST compensation, and strengthening cooperative federalism.
• State representatives raised concerns regarding the adequacy and timely transfer of funds from the Centre, particularly for centrally sponsored schemes.
• Deliberations focused on streamlining the Goods and Services Tax (GST) compensation mechanism and addressing revenue shortfalls experienced by some states.
• The Council emphasized the need for greater consultation between the Centre and states on policy formulation and resource allocation.
• This meeting underscored the ongoing efforts to foster a more balanced and collaborative Centre-State relationship in India's federal structure.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Comparison of the Indian constitutional scheme with that of other countries
prelimsPoints: The Inter-State Council is established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution.
It is a recommendatory body to investigate and discuss subjects of common interest between the Centre and states.
The Council is chaired by the Prime Minister.
Fiscal devolution refers to the transfer of financial powers and resources from the central government to state governments.
GST compensation cess was levied to compensate states for revenue losses arising from the implementation of GST.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of the Inter-State Council in strengthening cooperative federalism in India.
Analyze the key challenges in fiscal devolution and Centre-State financial relations, particularly concerning GST compensation and centrally sponsored schemes.
Evaluate the mechanisms for dispute resolution between the Centre and states in India's federal structure.
Examine how greater consultation and collaboration between the Centre and states can lead to more effective governance and policy implementation.
GS2.imageDescription: A map of India with interconnected gears, symbolizing the federal structure and Centre-State cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The Sarkaria Commission on Centre-State Relations (1983) strongly recommended the establishment of a permanent Inter-State Council.

GS2.headline: India-Sri Lanka Maritime Security Dialogue Reinforces Regional Stability
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka held their annual Maritime Security Dialogue, focusing on enhanced cooperation to ensure peace and stability in the Indian Ocean Region.
• Discussions covered joint patrolling, intelligence sharing, capacity building for maritime law enforcement, and addressing issues like illegal fishing and drug trafficking.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to upholding international maritime law and freedom of navigation in critical sea lanes.
• The dialogue also explored avenues for greater collaboration in disaster response and humanitarian assistance in the region.
• This continued engagement highlights the importance of bilateral ties in securing the shared maritime domain and promoting regional prosperity.

Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests

The Palk Strait and Gulf of Mannar are significant maritime areas between the two countries.
India's SAGAR (Security And Growth for All in the Region) doctrine emphasizes maritime cooperation.
Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated (IUU) fishing is a major concern.
Joint exercises like SLINEX (Sri Lanka-India Naval Exercise) are conducted.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Ambitious 'Green Growth 2.0' Roadmap on World Environment Day
GS3.explainer:
• On World Environment Day 2025, India launched an enhanced 'Green Growth 2.0' roadmap, signaling a deeper commitment to sustainable development and climate action.
• The new roadmap emphasizes scaling up Mission LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) initiatives, promoting behavioral changes for sustainable living across all sections of society.
• Significant focus is placed on expanding the Green Credit Program, incentivizing voluntary environmental actions by individuals and organizations through a market-based mechanism.
• India reiterated its accelerated targets for renewable energy deployment, aiming for substantial increase in non-fossil fuel capacity by 2030, aligning with its Net Zero commitments.
• The plan also includes enhanced international cooperation for climate finance and technology transfer, positioning India as a leader in global environmental stewardship.

Climate change
Sustainable development

Mission LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) was launched by India in 2022.
The Green Credit Program aims to incentivize environmental actions.
India's updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) include targets for renewable energy capacity.
The concept of 'Green Growth' integrates economic growth with environmental sustainability.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in global climate action and environmental conservation.
Analyze the potential of Mission LiFE and Green Credit Program in fostering sustainable behavioral changes.

GS3.headline: Gati Shakti Master Plan Accelerates Multi-Modal Infrastructure Development, Boosts Economic Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry reported significant progress under the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan, accelerating multi-modal infrastructure projects across the country.
• The integrated planning platform has enabled seamless coordination among various ministries, reducing project delays and optimizing resource allocation for critical infrastructure.
• Key achievements include faster execution of highway projects, expansion of railway networks, development of new ports, and enhanced logistics efficiency.
• The plan's emphasis on data-driven decision-making and geospatial mapping has led to more efficient project alignment and reduced environmental impact.
• Economic analysts attribute the improved infrastructure to a positive impact on manufacturing, exports, and overall economic growth, creating new employment opportunities.

The objective is to reduce logistics costs and improve global competitiveness.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD) for Future Space Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
• This test marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aiming to drastically reduce the cost of space access.
• The RLV-TD demonstrated advanced hypersonic flight capabilities, autonomous landing, and re-entry maneuvers, validating critical technologies.
• ISRO Chairman highlighted the indigenous development of these technologies, underscoring India's growing self-reliance in cutting-edge space research.
• The successful demonstration paves the way for future operational reusable launch vehicles, enhancing India's competitive edge in the global space market.

Space

GS3.headline: Project Tiger Completes 52 Years, India Reports Steady Increase in Big Cat Population
GS3.explainer:
• India today celebrated 52 years of Project Tiger, a landmark conservation program, with the release of a report indicating a continued steady increase in the country's tiger population.
• The latest census data highlights the success of stringent conservation measures, habitat protection, and anti-poaching efforts across various tiger reserves.
• Despite the overall positive trend, the report flagged concerns regarding human-wildlife conflict in buffer zones and the need for enhanced corridor connectivity.
• Union Environment Minister reiterated India's commitment to the 'Save Tiger' mission, emphasizing community participation and sustainable tourism practices.
• India currently hosts over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, solidifying its position as a global leader in tiger conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
The latest tiger census (2022) reported 3,682 tigers in India.
India accounts for approximately 70% of the world's tiger population.
National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is the statutory body for tiger conservation.
Tiger reserves are legally established under the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key strategies and successes of Project Tiger over the past five decades in conserving the tiger population in India.
Analyze the ongoing challenges in tiger conservation, including human-wildlife conflict and habitat fragmentation, and suggest mitigation measures.
Evaluate the role of community participation and eco-tourism in promoting sustainable tiger conservation efforts.
Examine how tiger conservation contributes to broader biodiversity protection and ecological balance in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger walking through dense forest foliage.
GS3.trivia: The tiger (Panthera tigris) is India's national animal.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Agri-Tech Start-ups with New Incubation Fund to Modernize Farming
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced the establishment of a new incubation fund dedicated to supporting agri-tech start-ups across India.
• The fund aims to foster innovation in agriculture, encouraging the development of solutions for precision farming, supply chain optimization, and market linkages.
• This initiative is part of the government's broader strategy to modernize the agricultural sector, enhance farmer income, and ensure food security through technological intervention.
• Selected start-ups will receive financial assistance, mentorship, and access to agricultural research facilities and farmer networks.
• The move is expected to attract more young entrepreneurs to agriculture, driving sustainable and efficient farming practices.

GS3.trivia: India's agricultural sector contributes significantly to its GDP and employs a large portion of the workforce.

GS3.headline: Cybersecurity Framework Strengthened Amidst Rising Digital Threats to Critical Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) announced a significant upgrade to India's cybersecurity framework following a surge in digital threats.
• The enhanced framework focuses on real-time threat intelligence sharing, improved incident response protocols, and deeper public-private partnerships in critical sectors.
• New guidelines were issued for safeguarding critical infrastructure, including energy, finance, and telecommunications, against sophisticated cyber-attacks.
• The government also launched a nationwide awareness campaign to educate organizations and individuals about best practices for cyber hygiene and data protection.
• These measures aim to fortify India's digital defenses and ensure the resilience of its vital services in an increasingly interconnected world.

Evaluate the importance of cyber hygiene and digital literacy for citizens in bolstering national cybersecurity.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital shield icon overlaying a network of interconnected servers, symbolizing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top countries experiencing a high volume of cyber-attacks annually.

GS3.headline: India Explores Green Hydrogen Export Potential with New Production Incentives
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced new production-linked incentives (PLI) for green hydrogen and electrolyzer manufacturing.
• This move aims to boost domestic production capabilities and position India as a global hub for green hydrogen export.
• Green hydrogen, produced using renewable energy, is seen as a crucial component of India's energy transition strategy towards achieving Net Zero emissions.
• The incentives are designed to attract significant investments in the sector, create jobs, and reduce the cost of green hydrogen production.
• Experts believe that with its abundant renewable energy resources, India has the potential to become a leading player in the global green hydrogen economy.

GS3.headline: Monsoon Preparedness Review Highlights Focus on Climate-Resilient Agriculture
GS3.explainer:
• The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) conducted a review of monsoon preparedness, emphasizing the need for climate-resilient agricultural practices.
• With predictions of varying rainfall patterns, states were urged to implement early warning systems and promote drought-resistant crop varieties.
• Focus was also placed on enhancing water conservation measures, including rainwater harvesting and efficient irrigation techniques, to mitigate flood and drought risks.
• The review highlighted the importance of inter-agency coordination and community-level preparedness for effective disaster management during the monsoon season.
• This proactive approach aims to safeguard agricultural livelihoods and ensure food security amidst changing climatic conditions.

GS3.headline: RBI Mandates Enhanced Cyber Security Measures for Cooperative Banks
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) issued new directives mandating enhanced cybersecurity measures for all cooperative banks, including urban and rural cooperative banks.
• The move comes amidst increasing digitization of banking services and a rise in cyber-attacks targeting financial institutions.
• The directives include implementing robust IT governance frameworks, conducting regular cybersecurity audits, and establishing dedicated cyber incident response teams.
• RBI emphasized the importance of safeguarding customer data and ensuring the resilience of banking operations against evolving cyber threats.
• This regulatory push aims to strengthen the overall financial stability and security of the cooperative banking sector.

GS3.headline: Indus-X Showcases Defence Innovation, Fostering US-India Collaboration
GS3.explainer:
• The second edition of the 'Indus-X Summit' was held, bringing together defence innovators, start-ups, and industry leaders from India and the United States.
• The event showcased cutting-edge technologies and solutions in areas such as artificial intelligence, unmanned systems, cyber security, and advanced materials for defence applications.
• Indus-X aims to facilitate joint research and development, co-production, and co-development initiatives between the two countries.
• This collaboration is a key component of the iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies) framework, fostering a robust defence industrial ecosystem.
• The summit underscored the growing strategic alignment between India and the US in defence and technology, promoting interoperability and innovation.

GS3.headline: Urban Local Bodies Adopt Innovative Waste Management Solutions for Swachh Bharat Mission
GS3.explainer:
• Several Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) across India were recognized for implementing innovative waste management solutions under the Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban 2.0.
• These initiatives include advanced waste-to-energy plants, decentralized composting units, and robust segregation-at-source programs.
• Focus has been on adopting circular economy principles, maximizing resource recovery, and minimizing landfill dependence.
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs highlighted successful models involving public-private partnerships and community participation.
• These efforts are crucial for achieving the goals of a 'Garbage-Free India' and improving urban sanitation and environmental health.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Embraces Digital Transformation for Post-Pandemic Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism highlighted the ongoing digital transformation in India's tourism sector, aiming for robust post-pandemic growth.
• Initiatives include the development of a national digital tourism platform, promoting virtual tours, and leveraging AI for personalized travel experiences.
• Focus has been on enhancing online booking systems, digital payment infrastructure, and seamless connectivity for tourists.
• The 'Dekho Apna Desh' initiative continues to encourage domestic tourism, supported by digital marketing campaigns and unique experiential offerings.
• This digital push is expected to attract a new generation of travelers, improve service quality, and boost employment in the hospitality industry.

GS3.headline: Startup India Initiative Expands to Tier 2 & 3 Cities, Fostering Local Entrepreneurship
GS3.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) announced the expansion of the Startup India initiative to aggressively promote entrepreneurship in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
• This expansion includes setting up more incubation centers, providing mentorship, and offering seed funding specifically tailored for local innovations.
• The aim is to decentralize the startup ecosystem, tap into regional talent, and address specific local challenges through innovative solutions.
• Government programs will focus on creating a supportive regulatory environment and facilitating market access for emerging startups in smaller cities.
• This move is expected to boost local economies, create jobs, and foster a culture of innovation beyond major metropolitan areas.
